title: Organ Health Report
description: A periodic health snapshot of an AI operating system, treated as a body of organs. Every degraded organ must open a task, not just change a dashboard colour.

report_id.description: Stable unique id for this report, e.g. 'ohr-2026-07-04-w27'.

period.description: Human-readable window this report covers, e.g. '2026-06-28..2026-07-04'.

organs.description: One entry per monitored organ. All nine should normally appear.

organ.description: Which organ this entry reports on.

status.description: Health of this organ. 'unknown' means the check itself did not report — treat as bad news.

metrics.description: Key numbers behind the status. Prefer queues and timestamps over totals.

queue_depth.description: Depth of the relevant queue (promotion, approval, etc.).

oldest_item_age.description: Age of the oldest un-drained item, e.g. '26h'.

last_success_at.description: Last successful run/capture/heartbeat for this organ.

threshold_tripped.description: Which threshold was crossed, stated plainly. Null when status is ok.

recovery_action.description: The specific next step to restore health (ties to ch. 10z recovery actions).

owner_role.description: Role that can perform the recovery — a role, not a person's name.

task_opened.description: Whether a work-queue task was opened for this degradation. Must be true for any non-ok status.

task_ref.description: Reference to the opened task in the work/approval queue. Null if none.

overall_status.description: Roll-up across all organs. Worst organ wins; 'unknown' if any check failed to report.

next_report_due.description: When the next report must arrive. A missing report by this time is itself a degradation of the observability organ.
